title: Receipt
description: Proof artifact emitted by every HeyDoc MCP server tool call. Attests that a live operational fact was retrieved from a real (or explicitly mock) source rather than generated by the LLM. The request_id is the cross-reference key stored in EvidenceNode.supports[].ref — it is what the verification layer checks to confirm a claim is grounded.

request_id.description: Unique identifier for this specific tool call. Generated by the MCP server. This exact value MUST be stored in EvidenceNode.supports[].ref for any claim derived from this call. Convention: <server-prefix>-<epoch-ms>-<random-7-chars>, e.g. 'doc-1719014400000-a3f7b2c'.

timestamp_utc.description: ISO 8601 UTC datetime of when the tool call was executed. Must be UTC — no local timezone offsets. Example: '2026-06-23T00:00:00Z'.

upstream.description: Identifier of the service or data source that answered this call. Set to the vendor/service name in live mode, or 'stub' during development. Examples: 'NCTS-AU' (SNOMED/ICD), 'docs-local' (docs MCP), 'MIMS-AU' (pharmacology), 'HI-Service-AU' (IHI lookup). Never leave blank — 'stub' is the correct dev value, not an empty string.

mode.description: Operating mode of the server that produced this receipt. The verification layer (verifier.js) MUST flag any receipt with mode='mock' in a production context. 'live' = real upstream call made. 'dry_run' = query validated but upstream not called. 'mock' = canned stub response, no upstream involved.

tool.description: Optional. The MCP tool name that was called, for audit trail clarity. Examples: 'docs_cite', 'terminology_lookup', 'identity_lookup_ihi', 'pharm_check'.

server.description: Optional. The MCP server name that handled the call. Matches the key in mcpServers.template.json.

latency_ms.description: Optional. Round-trip latency in milliseconds for the upstream call. Emitted by live MCP servers; omit in mock/dry_run where latency is meaningless. Useful for SLO monitoring.

correlation_id.description: Optional. Session or encounter correlation ID. Links this receipt back to a specific encounter for audit log joining. Should match ContextGraph.encounter_id when called during an active session.

error.description: Optional. Present only when the tool call completed but returned a non-fatal error condition. Fatal errors should be thrown as exceptions, not captured here.

retryable.description: Whether the caller should retry this call. True for transient errors (timeout, rate limit). False for permanent errors (not found, unauthorised).

cross_references: EvidenceNode.supports[].kind must be 'live_data_receipt' when referencing a Receipt
TerminologyLookup.receipt — embeds a Receipt object directly
ContextPacket.receipts[] — array of Receipt objects injected into trunk context
VerificationReport.checks — verifier checks each EvidenceNode ref against receipt store
PharmCheck.receipt — embeds a Receipt object for the pharmacology firewall call
verifier_rules: Any EvidenceNode with kind='live_data_receipt' must have a Receipt with matching request_id in the session receipt store
mode='mock' receipts are valid in development but must be flagged in verification reports
mode='mock' MUST be blocked in production by the verification layer
Missing receipt for a live-data claim = verification FAIL
_integration_notes.server_implementation: Each MCP server generates receipts via its local receipt() function. This schema formalises the shape that was previously copy-pasted across mcp/servers/docs/index.js, mcp/servers/terminology/index.js, and mcp/servers/identity-au/index.js. All three should be updated to validate their receipt output against this schema.
